en.extract:
The Somali pigeon (Columba oliviae) or Somali stock dove is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is endemic to Somalia. The IUCN Redlist identified a possible threat to the species from the speckled pigeon (Columba guinea). C. oliviae is found mostly on the ground, where it often forages, most frequently in rock along arid coasts.


Taxonomy
The Somali pigeon is placed in the Oenas sub-group within the genus Columba alongside the stock dove and yellow-eyed pigeon, based on their similar mauve heads and wing markings, but believed to be more closely related to the latter overall.


Description
C. oliviae is a mid-sized pigeon with pale grey coloration, a mauve cap, yellow eyes and an iridescent collar with bronze-green tones. It has a bluish-gray rump, dark wing edges and dark bands along its tail; all of these features are most clearly seen in flight. Its range overlaps with that of the much darker and more densely spotted speckled pigeon, which is rapidly expanding into the Somali pigeon's territory. Unlike various species of collared doves with which its range overlaps, it lacks a characteristic dark band on the hind-neck. It may also be confused with feral pigeons (C. livia), but the Somali pigeon is smaller and less dark than C. livia.


Vocalizations
As with many other aspects of the species, the Somali pigeon's vocalizations are not well known, but have described as a "wuk-wuk-wuk-ooooh". It also produces other pigeon-like noises, including cooing sounds.


Distribution and habitat
The exact distribution is unclear, but its range encompasses much of northeast Somalia, especially along the coast, including both the country proper and the unrecognized state of Somaliland. It is non-migratory. It is a mostly ground-dwelling bird, found in arid, coastal environments, especially on cliffs and hills, usually found in areas with little vegetation. It is typically seen at altitudes of 75 metres (246 ft) to 750 metres (2,460 ft), but has been recorded as high as 1,425 metres (4,675 ft).


Behavior and ecology
The Somali pigeon's range overlaps with that of other doves, including the speckled pigeon. While uncommon, it can be found in pairs or small flocks. It is often seen feeding on the ground, consuming seeds, cultivated grains and berries.


Status
The exact population size and trend is unknown. However, there are no laws in effect to protect it and it is considered urgently in need of some type of population survey. It may also be experiencing competition from the rapidly expanding speckled pigeon, although further study is still needed to be certain. Plans to organize research on the species have been made. 
As of February 2022, on eBird, a site that collects bird population data based on user-provided information, only six observations of the Somali pigeon have been made in over four hundred thousand checklists submitted throughout the African continent (and over sixty million worldwide).


In culture
The bird is featured on the one shilling coin's obverse of the internationally unrecognized East African republic of Somaliland's official currency.


References


External links
Species profile on eBird (includes images)

The '''Somali pigeon''' ('''''Columba oliviae''''') or '''Somali stock dove''' is a species of bird in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Somalia]]. The [[IUCN Redlist]] identified a possible threat to the species from the [[speckled pigeon]] (''Columba guinea'').<ref name="iucn" /> ''C. oliviae'' is found mostly on the ground, where it often forages, most frequently in rock along arid coasts.

==Taxonomy==
The Somali pigeon is placed in the ''Oenas'' sub-group within the [[genus]] ''[[Columba (genus)|Columba]]'' alongside the [[stock dove]] and [[yellow-eyed pigeon]], based on their similar mauve heads and wing markings, but believed to be more closely related to the latter overall.<ref>{{cite journal|title=Taxonomy of the Genus ''Columba''|url=http://www.macaronesian.org/assets/files/file-e7a51afe4001ca.pdf|author=D. Goodwin|access-date=19 February 2022|journal=Bulletin of the British Museum}}</ref>

==Description==
''C. oliviae'' is a mid-sized pigeon with pale grey coloration, a mauve cap, yellow eyes and an iridescent collar with bronze-green tones. It has a bluish-gray rump, dark wing edges and dark bands along its tail; all of these features are most clearly seen in flight. Its range overlaps with that of the much darker and more densely spotted speckled pigeon, which is rapidly expanding into the Somali pigeon's territory. Unlike various species of [[Ring dove (disambiguation)|collared doves]] with which its range overlaps, it lacks a characteristic dark band on the hind-neck.<ref name="ebird"/> It may also be confused with [[feral pigeons]] (''C. livia''), but the Somali pigeon is smaller and less dark than ''C. livia''.<ref name="birdlife"/>

===Vocalizations===
As with many other aspects of the species, the Somali pigeon's vocalizations are not well known, but have described as a "''wuk-wuk-wuk-ooooh''". It also produces other pigeon-like noises, including cooing sounds.<ref name="ebird"/><ref name="birdlife"/>

==Distribution and habitat==
The exact distribution is unclear, but its range encompasses much of northeast Somalia, especially along the coast, including both the country proper and the [[list of states with limited recognition|unrecognized]] state of [[Somaliland]]. It is non-migratory.<ref name="ebird">{{cite web|title=Somali Pigeon|url=https://ebird.org/species/sompig1|website=eBird|access-date=19 February 2022}}</ref><ref name="birdlife">{{cite web|title=Somali Pigeon ''Columba oliviae''|url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/somali-pigeon-columba-oliviae/text|website=BirdLife International|access-date=19 February 2022}}</ref> It is a mostly ground-dwelling bird, found in arid, coastal environments, especially on cliffs and hills, usually found in areas with little vegetation. It is typically seen at altitudes of {{Convert|75|m|ft}} to {{Convert|750|m|ft}},<ref name="iucn" /> but has been recorded as high as {{Convert|1425|m|ft}}.<ref name="birdlife"/>

==Behavior and ecology==
The Somali pigeon's range overlaps with that of other doves, including the speckled pigeon. While uncommon, it can be found in pairs or small flocks.<ref name="ebird"/> It is often seen feeding on the ground, consuming [[seeds]], cultivated [[grains]] and [[berries]].<ref name="birdlife"/><ref name="iucn" />

==Status==
The exact population size and trend is unknown. However, there are no laws in effect to protect it and it is considered urgently in need of some type of population survey.<ref name="birdlife"/><!--Source duplicated for DYK--> It may also be experiencing competition from the rapidly expanding speckled pigeon, although further study is still needed to be certain. Plans to organize research on the species have been made.<ref name="iucn" /><ref name="ebird"/><ref name="birdlife"/> 

As of February 2022, on [[eBird]], a site that collects bird population data based on user-provided information, only six observations of the Somali pigeon have been made<ref name="ebird"/> in over four hundred thousand checklists submitted throughout the [[African continent]]<ref>{{cite web|title=Africa|url=https://ebird.org/region/af/activity?yr=all|website=eBird|access-date=22 February 2022}}</ref> (and over sixty million worldwide<ref>{{cite web|title=World|url=https://ebird.org/region/world?yr=all|website=eBird|access-date=22 February 2022}}</ref>).

==In culture==
The bird is featured on the [[Somaliland shilling#coins|one shilling coin]]'s [[obverse]] of the internationally unrecognized [[East Africa|East African]] republic of Somaliland's official currency.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://africa.worldofcoins.eu/af-sol.htm |title=Somaliland and the 1 Shilling Coin |last=Baker |first=Paul |publisher=Token Publishing, UK |magazine=Coin News |date=January 2004 |access-date=6 March 2022}}</ref>

zh.extract:
索马里岩鸽（学名：Columba oliviae[Columba oliviae]），是鸽形目鸠鸽科鸽属的一种鸟类。主要分布于索马里东北部沿海地带。


特征
索马里岩鸽体长约28厘米，其羽毛整体呈现薰衣草灰色，背部与翅膀略微揉合棕色，而下背至臀部的蓝灰色调则相对较深。该物种的初级飞羽尖端为黑色，黑色的尾羽上横贯着一条灰色中央带。头部的前额、顶冠及颈后被覆着薰衣草粉色，颈后侧面具有一块带绿色光泽的铜棕色斑块。


习性
索马里岩鸽主要栖息在海拔 750米以下（最高记录达1425米）的干旱岩石山区及砂岩悬崖。它们极度耐旱，偏爱植被稀疏且几乎无水源的环境。具有季节性迁徙习惯。主要在地面活动，以种子、谷物和浆果为食。


流行文化
索马里岩鸽的形象出现在国际不承认的国家索马里兰官方货币一先令硬币的正面。
